seo.title: New York Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in New York — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license new york, dental assistant certification new york, RDA new york, DANB CDA new york, dental assistant radiography new york

board.name: New York State Education Department
board.mailing_address:
New York State Education Department
Office of the Professions
PO Box 22063
Albany, NY 12201

license_types.title: Certified Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in New York.

steps.title: Meet Education and Experience Requirements
steps.description: Fulfill the educational requirements for a Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) license, which typically includes graduation from a New York State registered dental assisting program or a program deemed substantially equivalent, or documented dental assisting experience, along with a high school diploma or equivalent.

steps.title: Complete Mandated Courses
steps.description: Successfully complete a Department-approved course in dental radiography, infection control, and a course in child abuse identification and reporting, as required for all healthcare professionals in New York.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Pass the New York State Dental Assisting written examination (NYSDALE), the Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) examination administered by the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB), and a Department-approved clinical performance examination (e.g., DANB Clinical Chairside Assisting - CCA).

steps.title: Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Create a NY.gov account and log in to the NYSED.gov Online Applications system. Complete and submit Form 1, Application for Licensure, along with the required $50 licensing fee. This application initiates your licensure process with the Office of the Professions.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Ensure all necessary supporting documents, such as academic transcripts, official examination results from DANB and NYSED, and verification of course completion, are sent directly from the issuing institutions or agencies to the Office of the Professions as per their instructions found on the NYSED.gov portal.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Certified Dental Assistant through the New York State Education Department by certification.

faqs.question: Does New York require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, New York requires Certified Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in New York.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in New York?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $60. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Certified Dental Assistant through the New York State Education Department by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in New York
hero.dek: To apply for a Registered Dental Assistant license in New York, create a NY.gov account and submit your application and supporting documentation through the NYSED.gov Online Applications system.
